goal: Generate and validate a vtk.js scene whose prompt, code, and workflow input are all ready for corpus review
title: vtk.js Scene Validation
abstract: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: generic. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk. | Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render().. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic. | If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts. | Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors..
